skill.name: better-auth-best-practices
skill.description: Guides Better Auth setup for TypeScript projects, including server configuration, clients, database adapters, sessions, plugins, and environment variables.
skill.summary: Apply Better Auth best practices for secure TypeScript authentication setup.
skill.author: better-auth

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, credential, cryptography, and combined-risk patterns. Review found the command hits are Markdown examples for Better Auth setup, the URLs point to Better Auth documentation and GitHub, and no credential file access, weak cryptography implementation, obfuscation, or prompt injection was found. The skill is publishable with a medium warning because it instructs users to run package manager and migration commands in their own project.

medium_findings.title: Project Setup Commands Require User Trust
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends npm and npx commands for installing Better Auth, running migrations, generating schemas, and configuring MCP. These are not hidden execution paths, but they can change a local project and download packages, so users should review package sources and run them only in trusted repositories.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly documented in Markdown and would be user-approved setup actions, not automatic execution. The operational risk is real because npm and npx can fetch and run package code.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The skill links to Better Auth documentation, an LLMs text file, and GitHub resources. These links support the intended task and do not show data exfiltration or suspicious endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation references for the Better Auth project. No code sends local data or secrets to these URLs.

low_findings.title: Credential And Cryptography Scanner Matches Are Semantic False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged browser credential files and weak cryptography, but the referenced lines describe Better Auth options such as secure cookies, cross-subdomain cookies, hook context, and setup metadata. No file reads, browser credential paths, or cryptographic implementation were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain configuration names and descriptive text only. There is no executable code, credential file path, hashing implementation, or data access behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dismissed Static Heuristic: Code Execution Plus Network Plus Credential Access
dangerous_patterns.description: The combined heuristic was triggered by Markdown backticks, documentation URLs, and authentication-related option names. Manual review found no automatic command execution, no credential file access, no exfiltration path, and no prompt injection attempt.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious categories co-occur in one documentation file, but the semantic context is a Better Auth setup guide. No malicious control flow or hidden instructions were present.

content.value_statement: Authentication setup is easy to misconfigure across server, client, database, and session layers. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused Better Auth guidance for secure TypeScript projects.
actual_capabilities: Guides Better Auth server configuration with environment variables, base URLs, secrets, and route handlers.
Explains database adapter choices for direct connections, Prisma, Drizzle, MongoDB, SQLite, and related schema generation.
Summarizes session storage, cookie cache strategies, expiration settings, and secure cookie options.
Lists plugin import patterns and common plugins for two-factor auth, passkeys, organizations, SSO, JWT, and API keys.
Covers client setup for React, Vue, Svelte, Solid, and vanilla Better Auth clients.
Highlights common mistakes around model names, plugin schema updates, secondary storage, and stateless sessions.
limitations: It does not replace the official Better Auth documentation or version-specific API references.
It does not verify a project database schema or run migrations by itself.
It does not audit application-specific authorization rules or business logic.
It may require updates when Better Auth changes command names, options, or plugin APIs.

use_cases.title: Set up a new TypeScript authentication stack
use_cases.description: Create a Better Auth configuration with environment variables, database setup, route handlers, and a matching client.

use_cases.title: Review session and cookie behavior
use_cases.description: Compare database sessions, secondary storage, cookie cache modes, expiration settings, and secure cookie requirements.

use_cases.title: Add Better Auth plugins correctly
use_cases.description: Choose plugin import paths, update schema generation steps, and avoid common plugin configuration mistakes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic setup plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Better Auth best practices skill to outline the files, environment variables, and setup commands I need for a new TypeScript app.

prompt_templates.title: Review my auth configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Better Auth best practices skill to review my auth.ts configuration for environment variables, database adapter setup, sessions, and secure cookie settings.

prompt_templates.title: Plan plugin adoption
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Better Auth best practices skill to help me add two-factor auth and passkey support. Include schema generation, import paths, client plugins, and migration steps.

prompt_templates.title: Audit production readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Better Auth best practices skill to assess my production readiness across trusted origins, CSRF settings, rate limiting, secondary storage, session persistence, and OAuth account linking.

output_examples.input: I need Better Auth in a new TypeScript app.
output_examples.output: A setup checklist covering package installation, required environment variables, auth configuration, route handler placement, database migration, and a health check endpoint.

output_examples.input: My sessions are not appearing in the database.
output_examples.output: A diagnostic explanation of secondary storage priority, session database persistence settings, cookie cache behavior, and the settings to check first.

output_examples.input: I added a plugin and authentication stopped working.
output_examples.output: A review path that checks plugin import paths, server and client plugin placement, schema regeneration, migrations, and common model-name mismatches.

best_practices: Keep Better Auth secrets and base URLs in environment variables unless a deployment requires explicit configuration.
Re-run the Better Auth CLI after adding or changing plugins so database schema stays aligned.
Review secure cookies, trusted origins, CSRF checks, origin checks, and rate limits before production release.
anti_patterns: Do not disable CSRF or origin checks unless you have a documented and reviewed compensating control.
Do not use database table names where Better Auth expects adapter model names.
Do not assume sessions are stored in the database when secondary storage or cookie cache settings are enabled.

faq.question: Does this skill run Better Auth commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents commands and setup steps. Users should approve and run commands in a trusted project environment.

faq.question: Can it configure both server and client code?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers server auth configuration, route handlers, and client setup for supported Better Auth clients.

faq.question: Does it support database adapters?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains direct database connections and ORM adapters such as Prisma, Drizzle, and MongoDB.

faq.question: Can it help with OAuth and social login?
faq.answer: Yes. It points to Better Auth social provider configuration and account linking options.

faq.question: Does it replace the Better Auth docs?
faq.answer: No. It is a compact guide. Users should still verify current APIs in the official documentation.

faq.question: What security settings does it highlight?
faq.answer: It highlights secure cookies, trusted origins, CSRF checks, origin checks, rate limiting, and session storage choices.
